content:
You’ve Got New Customers. And They’re Not Human

6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Generated by GeminiAbout 1.5 years ago, OpenAI introduced Operator, a since deprecated product that was basically a predecessor to what we now call agents. Its functionality was bare bones compared to what today’s agents are capable of. It could book hotels, reserve a table at a restaurant, place a grocery order. If you could perform the task through a web browser, Operator could do it for you.
When it came out, Igor Pogany from The AI Advantage shared what became one of my most memorable agents gone rogue anecdotes. He had been successfully testing out Operator to order groceries. But when he reworked his prompt to place an order with a different supermarket, not only did his order arrive at a different address, it included 30 bananas instead of 5.
80 euros and one confused stranger later, Igor arrived at the conclusion that the mistake was in his prompt. But what if it wasn’t? Models often fail in weird and fascinating ways, so it’s easy to blame them when things go sideways. But according to the latest research on AI web agents, the websites are also to blame. So what’s a company to do?
How Websites Are Failing a New Target Audience
